claims.claim: Pipecat SimpleTextAggregator accumulates char-by-char, sets lookahead flag on sentence-ending punctuation, waits for next non-whitespace char, confirms via NLTK match_endofsentence; $29. example
claims.found: for char in text: self._text += char (l70-71); if self._needs_lookahead: if char.strip(): ... eos_marker = match_endofsentence(self._text) (l99-104); docstring: 'disambiguates cases like "$29." (not a sentence) vs "$29. Next"' (l84); self._needs_lookahead = True on SENTENCE_ENDING_PUNCTUATION (l117-119)
import nltk; from nltk.tokenize import sent_tokenize; punkt_tab download (string.py l24-44); def match_endofsentence at l125

claims.claim: Pipecat notes aggregation costs ~200-300ms latency per sentence waiting for sentence-ending punctuation token from LLM
claims.found: comment l498-503: 'This adds ~200-300ms of latency per sentence (waiting for the sentence-ending punctuation token from the LLM). Setting text_aggregation_mode=TextAggregationMode.TOKEN streams tokens directly.'

claims.claim: Rapida flushes complete sentences as TextToSpeechTextPacket on each LLM delta
claims.found: handleDeltaLocked appends delta.Text then extractSentencesAtBoundaryLocked (l231-232); emits internal_type.TextToSpeechTextPacket{...Text: complete} (l274-277)

claims.claim: Rapida deliberately does not consume trailing whitespace so next chunk keeps leading space, 'preventing TTS engines from merging words across sentence boundaries'
claims.found: comment l114-117: 'Whitespace after the boundary is intentionally NOT consumed so that it is preserved as a leading space in the next emitted chunk, preventing TTS engines from merging words across sentence boundaries.' pattern is (%s) join, no \s (l124)

claims.claim: LiveKit split_sentences uses <prd>/<stop> sentinels ported from StackOverflow heuristic, honoring abbreviations (Mr/Dr/Ph.D), acronyms, decimals, URLs
claims.found: comment l4 'rule based segmentation based on https://stackoverflow.com/a/31505798'; prefixes (Mr|St|Mrs|Ms|Dr) l12; Ph.D handling l33-34; acronyms l15; digits/decimal l17,28; websites com|net|org... l16; <prd>/<stop> throughout

claims.claim: BufferedTokenStream tokenizes once input buffer exceeds min_ctx_len (default 10), emits chunk once exceeds min_token_len/min_sentence_len (default 20)
claims.found: token_stream.py l39 'if len(self._in_buf) < self._min_ctx_len: return'; l56 'if len(self._out_buf) >= self._min_token_len:' emits TokenData. basic.py SentenceTokenizer defaults min_sentence_len=20 (l39), stream_context_len=10 (l40). (note: condition is >= not strictly 'exceeds', minor loose phrasing)

claims.claim: LiveKit JS uses identical <prd>/<stop> splitter with minLength = 20
claims.found: splitSentences(text, minLength = 20, ...) l8-10; replaceAll(prefixes,'$1<prd>') l29; '$1<stop> $2' l35

claims.claim: All three default to SENTENCE granularity; Pipecat/ElevenLabs expose TOKEN mode streaming tokens directly
claims.found: Pipecat text property returns AggregationType.SENTENCE (l47); TOKEN mode yields immediately (l64-67); elevenlabs comment text_aggregation_mode=TextAggregationMode.TOKEN streams tokens directly (l501)

summary: All 14 checkable assertions verified against the cloned source. Every code_ref (pipecat SimpleTextAggregator char-by-char lookahead + NLTK, elevenlabs ~200-300ms latency comment, Rapida Go multilingual boundary set + no-whitespace-consume comment, LiveKit Python/JS <prd>/<stop> splitter with min length 20 / ctx 10) sits at or within a few lines of the cited location and shows the claimed mechanism. One minor loose phrasing: 'exceeds min_ctx_len' is actually a >= comparison, not strictly greater-than, but not a factual error. No source-attributed numeric figures from the blog/youtube captures appear in the body (the sources list is thematic). PASS.
